author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Decision: Converting Phase 3 (sequential backward walk) into a parallel ready-queue engine using crossbeam channels. Architecture:

1. A 'ready queue' (MPMC channel) holds nodes whose in-degree is 0 (all consumers processed)
2. N worker threads pull nodes from the queue, call grad_fn.backward(), accumulate grads
3. After processing a node, decrement in-degrees of its inputs — if any reach 0, push to ready queue
4. Workers terminate when the channel is closed (all nodes processed)

The grads HashMap needs synchronization — using a Mutex<HashMap> since contention is low (each node touches different keys). The in-degree map also needs atomic decrements.

Using std::sync primitives (Mutex, condvar) since adding crossbeam dependency is overkill. Worker count = min(num_cpus, num_nodes).
